intro: The clearest split is that Algolia has a free tier while Coveo does not. Algolia is Hosted search API with typo-tolerance, faceting, and relevance tuning for e-commerce and SaaS; Coveo is AI-powered search and recommendations platform for enterprise portals, e-commerce, and support sites. The data points Algolia at e-commerce needing sub-50ms search and documentation sites with instant search, and Coveo at e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search. Pricing: Algolia is hybrid from $0.50/1K searches (Grow); Coveo is subscription. Lock-in is medium for Algolia and high for Coveo, with DX at 5/5 vs 4/5 and transparency at 4/5 vs 2/5. On listed strengths, Algolia emphasises e-commerce needing sub-50ms search and documentation sites with instant search, and Coveo emphasises e-commerce product search and documentation/knowledge base search. Both offer an enterprise tier for larger rollouts. Honest trade-off: Algolia is not for budget, simple projects; Coveo is not for simple projects, so confirm the fit first, then weigh the pivotal factor above against your budget and lock-in tolerance.
quick_take: Algolia is for e-commerce; Coveo is for e-commerce; decide based on whether a free tier matters.
verdict_a: Choose Algolia if you want to start on a free tier before paying and your workload matches e-commerce. Skip it if your context is budget, simple projects.
verdict_b: Choose Coveo if starting on a paid subscription upfront is acceptable and your workload matches e-commerce. Skip it if your context is simple projects.

faq.a: Entry pricing is listed as $0.50/1K searches (Grow) for Algolia and unknown for Coveo. Algolia offers a free tier while Coveo does not, so initial cost favors Algolia. Total cost at scale depends on usage and plan details that are not in this dataset, so confirm with each vendor.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Algolia to Coveo?
faq.a: Algolia is tagged with medium lock-in (medium — proprietary api, but instantsearch ui = open-source). Coveo is tagged with high lock-in (enterprise search platform, hard to migrate). At least one side is high lock-in, so budget migration time for re-implementing integrations.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, Algolia or Coveo?
faq.a: The dataset rates Algolia at 5/5 for developer experience and Coveo at 4/5, so Algolia scores higher. That score reflects overall polish rather than any single integration, so trial both against your stack before committing.

faq.q: Is Coveo a good alternative to Algolia?
faq.a: Yes for teams whose workload matches e-commerce, since both list the same fit. Coveo is flagged as not for simple projects, so confirm your context does not fall there. Note that moving from Algolia to Coveo means leaving a free tier behind.
